title: thymelaea tarton-raira subsp. thomasii
intro: Thymelaea tarton-raira subsp. thomasii, also known as the "Cretan spurge" or "Rock spurge", is a plant species in the family Thymelaeaceae, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Thymelaea tarton-raira subsp. thomasii include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or ointments, and has been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions and injuries.
